author: Dr. Phong Le, MD
department: Primary Care — Charm City Asian Health Center
content:
NEW PATIENT INTAKE — 4/18/2010, 10:15 AM
Patient: Nguyen, Lan Thi (DOB 9/22/1954, Age 55)
Insurance: Employer-sponsored (Baltimore Textile Co. employee plan, PPO)
Interpreter: Dr. Le speaks Vietnamese fluently with patient; no third-party interpreter needed.

CHIEF COMPLAINT:
Establish primary care. Workplace required follow-up for a positive PPD identified at annual screening (3/15/2010, induration 14 mm at 48 hours).

HPI:
Ms. Nguyen is a 55-year-old Vietnamese-speaking woman who immigrated from Hà Nam Province, Vietnam, in 1990. She has worked at Baltimore Textile Co. (Highlandtown garment factory) since 1990; promoted from sewer to floor manager in 1992. She has been generally healthy and has not had routine medical care since brief intake care at Hopkins Community Health in 1990 (immigration screening). The factory recently instituted annual occupational health TB screening, and her March 2010 PPD was positive at 14 mm. She has no symptoms of active TB — no cough, no hemoptysis, no fever, no night sweats, no weight loss. She received BCG vaccination as a child during a mass campaign in rural Vietnam (visible scar on left deltoid). She has no known prior TB testing.

General health concerns she raises: occasional mild headaches; some right knee discomfort after long shifts (she stands all day overseeing the floor); fatigue at the end of the work week. No chest pain, no dyspnea on her usual activity, no syncope. Periods became irregular several years ago; last period probably 2007 — she is post-menopausal. No vaginal bleeding since.

DIET / EXERCISE: Traditional Vietnamese diet at home. Walks to/from work (~15 min each way). Tai chi twice weekly at Vietnamese community center.

SMOKING: Former smoker — ~10 cigarettes/day from age 22 (1976 in Vietnam) until she quit in 2004 (~14 pack-years lifetime) after her husband's cardiac scare; no relapse since. Husband is a current smoker.

ALCOHOL: 1–2 drinks/day, typically rice wine or beer. Daily for many years. No binge drinking.

FAMILY HISTORY: Father died of MI at 68 (untreated HTN). Mother alive, age 82, with type 2 diabetes diagnosed in her 70s. Older brother heavy smoker, otherwise healthy. Younger brother T2DM. Sister healthy. Husband Hai (age 59) had NSTEMI in 2004 with triple-vessel CABG; doing well on medications.

SOCIAL: Married since 1973. Lives with husband and adult son Quang (age 30, IT job) in Highlandtown. Naturalized US citizen 1998.

PMH: None known. "Low blood" anemia as a teenager in Vietnam, treated with iron.

MEDICATIONS: None. Occasional acetaminophen for headaches; OTC ibuprofen 200 mg PRN for knee pain after long shifts.

ALLERGIES: Penicillin — childhood rash, never re-challenged. Otherwise NKDA.

GYN: G2P2 (both Vietnam, 1976 and 1980). Last pap unknown — probably never had one in US. Last mammogram never.

VITALS: BP 142/86 (right arm, sitting, after 5 min rest), HR 78, RR 14, T 98.4 F, SpO2 99% RA. Weight 58.2 kg (128 lb). Height 154 cm. BMI 24.5.

EXAM:
General: Pleasant, soft-spoken Vietnamese woman, no distress.
HEENT: PERRLA, oropharynx clear, no thyromegaly, no cervical lymphadenopathy.
CV: Regular rate and rhythm, normal S1/S2, no murmur. No JVD, no carotid bruit. Peripheral pulses 2+ throughout.
Lungs: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. No wheeze, rales, or rhonchi.
Abdomen: Soft, non-tender, non-distended. No organomegaly. No bruits.
MSK: Right knee with mild medial-joint-line tenderness, no effusion, full ROM with mild crepitus. Left knee unremarkable. Otherwise normal.
Skin: BCG scar left deltoid as described. No suspicious lesions. No rash.
Neuro: Grossly intact.

LABS TODAY (in-office):
- Random glucose 102 mg/dL
- BMP normal: Na 138, K 4.2, Cl 102, CO2 25, BUN 14, Cr 0.9 (eGFR 65 — borderline at her age), Glu 102
- CBC normal: WBC 6.4, Hgb 13.1, Plt 232
- Lipid panel pending
- Liver function panel pending
- HbA1c pending
- Urine pregnancy test (still doing as part of intake) — negative

IMPRESSION / PLAN:
1. LATENT TB INFECTION suspected based on positive PPD 14 mm in a Vietnamese-born adult, asymptomatic. Need to rule out active TB before considering treatment. → Will order chest X-ray today. → Refer to Pulmonary / ID for further evaluation (cannot do AFB sputum or QuantiFERON in-office). Patient agreed to follow up at Hopkins Bayview Pulmonary clinic.
2. HYPERTENSION, newly noted today — BP 142/86 in office, but only first-visit reading. Will recheck at f/u in 4 weeks. Counseled on low-sodium diet, weight maintenance, exercise.
3. SMOKING — former smoker, ~14 pack-years lifetime, quit 2004 after her husband's cardiac scare. Reinforced continued abstinence; counseled on secondhand-smoke exposure at home.
4. KNEE PAIN — mild medial OA per exam findings. Counseled on activity modification, weight maintenance, acetaminophen first-line. Use ibuprofen sparingly.
5. HEALTH MAINTENANCE — overdue for mammogram (refer); overdue for colonoscopy (refer for age-55 first colonoscopy); flu shot today (declined, mild needle anxiety today, will revisit); pneumovax not indicated yet at her age.
6. Annual physical scheduled in 12 months. Sooner f/u in 4 weeks for HTN recheck and to review pending labs.

Visit time: 60 minutes (Vietnamese-language visit, no formal interpreter time billing).
attestation:
Electronically signed by: Dr. Phong Le, MD
Charm City Asian Health Center (FQHC)
Date: 4/18/2010
